sections.title: Activating Brand Purpose & Sustainability
sections.subtitle: Driving behavior change and purchase intent at the moments that matter for colleagues, customers and consumers – especially at retail.
sections.highlightsDescription: Driving behavior change and purchase intent at the moments that matter for colleagues, customers and consumers – especially at retail.
highlights: Content, video, advertising, campaigns, events and experiences
Commerce (customer journeys, selling stories, brand and retail activation and tactical toolkits)

left.body:
## Brand Activation For Good

We like to take a design-sprint based approach to creative development, with the aim of enhancing co-creation and collaboration at every step.

So whether it's packaging, logos, visual identity systems, marketing collateral, consumer activations or brand experiences, we ensure that it's always delivered on brand, on budget and at speed – without the need for protracted meetings, rounds of revisions or ivory tower conversations.

All of our playbooks, frameworks, tools and ways of working are based on proprietary research, case studies and decades of consultation with some of the world's largest brand owners and retailers.

sections.quote: Coupled with the testing, it really brought to light the challenges we are facing and helped define the most impactful way to reach our shoppers through packaging, design and retail activation.
sections.author: Danielle Johnson
sections.imageAlt: Brand Activation For Good — thought leadership and framework overview

left.body:
## After each sprint you will have…

1. A Big Idea and activation strategy that maps back to purpose, sustainability strategy and the commercial drivers of your brand and business
2. Identified and prioritized the biggest intention-action gaps along the path to purchase – to drive the right behavior change at the moments that matter for your customers
3. Developed a range of potential designs, ideas, activations and experiences (that can all be tested in less than a week)
4. A commercial selling story and sell-in video to pitch those ideas to customers and key stakeholders
5. A robust tactical plan and toolkit

right.body: Discover how to develop more effective brand purpose and sustainability activation campaigns at retail

items.question: What kinds of campaigns do you typically design?
items.answer: Ominchannel retail activations, partnerships, digital campaigns, and experiential programs designed to drive both impact and sales.

items.question: How do you ensure sustainability messaging is commercially effective?
items.answer: We align sustainability commitments and claims with customer category strategy and shopper purchase drivers.

items.question: Do you create campaign concepts from scratch or build on client ideas?
items.answer: Both. We can originate fresh ideas or collaborate to evolve existing concepts or programs.

items.question: How do you integrate creative with cultural insights and sustainability frameworks?
items.answer: By combining insights from our research with proven frameworks like the 5Rs workshop and flywheel of impact

items.question: What deliverables are usually included?
items.answer: Depending on scope: campaign toolkits, creative assets, and playbooks for internal and retail teams to use and deploy. We generally don't handle campaign management and implementation.

items.question: How long does it take to develop an activation?
Do you test campaigns before launch?
items.answer: Yes. We often run concept and claims testing using our own white label testing platform to choose the best idea or refine messaging before we move to development or roll-out.

items.question: Can you support retail activation or B2B campaigns?
items.answer: Yes. We work across B2B, retail, and direct to consumer driven categories and markets.

items.question: Can you help us design sustainability activations that drive purchase intent?
items.answer: Yes. Retail Activation for Good if a workshop specifically designed to make sustainable shopping more accessible, aspirational and rewarding at point of purchase.

items.question: Do you collaborate with client agencies?
items.answer: Both — we can lead or integrate with your existing agency partners.
